title: Lastrea lauterbachii
taxonomy: Lastrea lauterbachii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Lastrea lauterbachii is further categorized under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The species is often found in shaded, humid areas with rich soil. By its classification, Lastrea lauterbachii is a type of epiphytic fern that thrives in environments with high humidity and limited sunlight.

intro: Lastrea lauterbachii, also known as the Lauterbach's fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with its health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and protect against cell damage.
